interactions.summary: Continuing the answer to Eric, Jiang speculates that great men may be telepaths in the functional sense: people able to read and control minds, step outside history, and direct it.
Answering Celine on how to model love, learning, and creation, Jiang says the model needs agency, freedom, social interaction, and compassion because human powers arise through other people.

Answering Eric's question about understanding human behavior and motivation, Jiang contrasts social science with literature and says Homer and Dante express human truths that cannot be mathematically captured, especially love and imagination.

Answering Celine's question about why humans destroy themselves if they want to love, create, and grow, Jiang says love and hate, creation and destruction are paired forces: blocked love becomes hate and blocked creation becomes destruction.

speaker_notes.note: All focus refs are Jiang's answers to named student questions; the student questions are paraphrased by Jiang rather than independently transcribed.

claims.claim: Jiang speculates that historical edge cases or great men can be understood as telepaths who can read and control minds.
He uses Putin as an example of someone who, without special elite background, amassed enough power that Russia moves according to his command.
He says Homer and Dante can only be explained as figures with God-given gifts, perhaps speaking for God, because they stepped outside history and directed it.
Answering Celine, he says love, learning, and creation can be modeled through agency, freedom, social interaction, and compassion.
He says human powers are relational: people cannot love themselves, create by themselves, or learn only from themselves.
He says social arrangements shape compassion: shared survival can produce cooperation, while competitive money incentives can make people hate each other.
He distinguishes social science, which tries to mathematize what drives people, from literature, which expresses truths that cannot be mathematically quantified.
He uses Priam kissing Achilles' hand for Hector's body as an example of human truth that cannot be mathematically expressed but can be written.
He says Dante makes explicit that humans are driven by love and the willingness to imagine, and that any AI model must treat those as underlying truths.
Answering Celine, he says love and hate are one force, and creation and destruction are one force; if love or creation cannot express itself, hate and destruction will.
He says centralized elites turn people into slaves, blocking love, creation, learning, and growth, which produces rebellion and social destruction.

signature_moments.moment: Putin is treated as almost a telepath because Russia moves the way he wants it to move.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the lecture's boldest great-man image and should survive the public read if possible.

signature_moments.moment: Love, creation, and learning require other people.
signature_moments.source_phrase: You can't love yourself. You have to love someone else. You can't create by yourself.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives the human-heart model a relational structure rather than an individualist one.

signature_moments.moment: An island teaches compassion; a bank with Ferrari incentives teaches hatred.
signature_moments.source_phrase: if I put you guys into a bank
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This vivid contrast shows how institutional design redirects human drives.

signature_moments.moment: Priam kissing Achilles' hand carries a truth no equation can hold.
signature_moments.source_phrase: You can never mathematically express it. You can only write about it.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It preserves the lecture's counterweight to mathematized psychohistory: literature knows what models cannot.

signature_moments.moment: Blocked love becomes hate; blocked creation becomes destruction.
signature_moments.source_phrase: love and hate are one force
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the sharp causal chain that explains why repression turns into collapse.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: A speculative name for great historical actors who seem able to read and control minds, stepping outside normal historical forces.
The freedom or liberty people need in order to love, create, learn, and grow.
A model variable measuring whether social conditions make people cooperate and work together.
The attempt to express human truths through literature when they cannot be fully quantified by social science.

glossary_terms.term: love and hate as one force
glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's model that hate is the expression of blocked love, just as destruction is blocked creation.

uncertainty_notes.note: The 'Putin as telepath' claim is framed by Jiang as an argument one can make, not as a settled literal claim.
ASR renders Priam as 'Prime'; public prose should silently use Priam if quoting is not exact.
